# CoinW.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with CoinW.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, or anti-hallucination rules.

CoinW.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for the CoinW REST and WebSocket APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, and socket patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 2.9.3.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 11.1.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, supported features, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/CoinW.Net/
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `CoinWRestClient` for REST and `CoinWSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to CoinW endpoints.
- Use `CoinWCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, for API key setup.
- Include `using CoinW.Net;` when using `CoinWCredentials`, `CoinWEnvironment`, or dependency injection options.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST methods return `WebCallResult<T>` or `WebCallResult`.
- WebSocket subscriptions return `CallResult<UpdateSubscription>`.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate REST or socket clients per request in production code.
- Prefer dependency injection for long-running services.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Do not call `.Result` or `.Wait()` on async methods.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`.
- Do not invent Binance API branches. CoinW has `SpotApi` and `FuturesApi`, not `UsdFuturesApi`, `CoinFuturesApi`, or `GeneralApi`.
- Use CoinW symbol formats: spot `BTC_USDT`, futures `BTC` for USDT perpetuals.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` interfaces from each exchange's `.SharedClient`.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package CoinW.Net
```

## Required Usings

Typical REST/trading code:

```csharp
using CoinW.Net;
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CoinW.Net.Enums;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Retry/error helper code may also need:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using CoinW.Net;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data:

```csharp
var client = new CoinWRestClient();

var tickers = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync();
if (!tickers.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickers.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(tickers.Data.Single(x => x.Symbol == "BTC_USDT").LastPrice);
```

Private account and trading endpoints:

```csharp
var client = new CoinWRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Socket client with credentials:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinWSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Environment:

```csharp
var live = new CoinWRestClient(options => options.Environment = CoinWEnvironment.Live);
```

## Dependency Injection

```csharp
services.AddCoinW(options =>
{
    options.Rest.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
    options.Socket.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
using CoinW.Net.Interfaces.Clients;

public class MyService
{
    private readonly ICoinWRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly ICoinWSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(ICoinWRestClient restClient, ICoinWSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var price = result.Data.LastPrice;
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<WebCallResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<WebCallResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    WebCallResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
CoinWRestClient
  .SpotApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
  .FuturesApi
    .ExchangeData
    .Account
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
```

WebSocket:

```text
CoinWSocketClient
  .SpotApi
    SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToAllTickerUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToPartialOrderBookUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToBalanceUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync
    .SharedClient
  .FuturesApi
    SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToIndexPriceUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToMarkPriceUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToFundingRateUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToPositionDetailUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToBalanceUpdatesAsync
    SubscribeToMarginConfigUpdatesAsync
    .SharedClient
```

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common REST tasks:

```text
Get spot tickers -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync()
Get spot symbols -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync()
Get spot order book -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("BTC_USDT")
Get spot klines -> client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("BTC_USDT", KlineInterval.OneMinute)
Get spot balances -> client.SpotApi.Account.GetBalancesAsync()
Get detailed spot balances -> client.SpotApi.Account.GetBalancesDetailsAsync()
Place spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Get spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.GetOrderAsync(orderId)
Cancel spot order -> client.SpotApi.Trading.CancelOrderAsync(orderId)
Get futures symbols -> client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync()
Get futures ticker -> client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC")
Get futures klines -> client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("BTC", FuturesKlineInterval.OneMinute, ...)
Get futures balances -> client.FuturesApi.Account.GetBalancesAsync()
Set futures margin mode -> client.FuturesApi.Account.SetMarginModeAsync(...)
Place futures order -> client.FuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...)
Get futures positions -> client.FuturesApi.Trading.GetPositionsAsync("BTC")
Close futures position -> client.FuturesApi.Trading.ClosePositionAsync(positionId, ...)
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Subscribe spot ticker -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync("BTC_USDT", handler)
Subscribe spot klines -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync("BTC_USDT", KlineIntervalStream.OneMinute, handler)
Subscribe spot order book -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync("BTC_USDT", handler)
Subscribe spot user order updates -> socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToOrderUpdatesAsync(handler)
Subscribe futures ticker -> socketClient.FuturesApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync("BTC", handler)
Subscribe futures klines -> socketClient.FuturesApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync("BTC", FuturesKlineIntervalStream.OneMinute, handler)
Subscribe futures user positions -> socketClient.FuturesApi.SubscribeToPositionUpdatesAsync(handler)
```

## Spot Order Example

```csharp
using CoinW.Net;
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CoinW.Net.Enums;

var client = new CoinWRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

var order = await client.SpotApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "BTC_USDT",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    type: OrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.001m,
    price: 50000m);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.OrderId);
```

For market buy orders, use `OrderType.Market` and pass either `quantity` or `quoteQuantity` depending on the desired CoinW order semantics.

## Futures Order Example

```csharp
using CoinW.Net;
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CoinW.Net.Enums;

var client = new CoinWRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinWCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET");
});

var order = await client.FuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH",
    side: PositionSide.Long,
    orderType: FuturesOrderType.Market,
    quantity: 1m,
    leverage: 5,
    quantityUnit: QuantityUnit.Contracts,
    marginType: MarginType.IsolatedMargin);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}
```

Use `PositionSide.Long` or `PositionSide.Short` to choose direction. Use `FuturesOrderType.Plan` with `price` for limit-style planned orders, and `ClosePositionAsync` or `CloseAllPositionsAsync` to close existing positions.

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CoinW.Net.Enums;

var socketClient = new CoinWSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.SpotApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    "BTC_USDT",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
```

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

```csharp
using CoinW.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;

ISpotTickerRestClient tickerClient = new CoinWRestClient().SpotApi.SharedClient;
var symbol = new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "BTC", "USDT");

var ticker = await tickerClient.GetSpotTickerAsync(new GetTickerRequest(symbol));
if (!ticker.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ticker.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(ticker.Data.LastPrice);
```

For shared socket subscriptions, keep the concrete socket client for teardown:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinWSocketClient();
ITickerSocketClient tickerSocket = socketClient.SpotApi.SharedClient;

var sub = await tickerSocket.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    new SubscribeTickerRequest(new SharedSymbol(TradingMode.Spot, "BTC", "USDT")),
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(sub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

## Symbol And Quantity Handling

For spot endpoints, CoinW symbols use an underscore (`BTC_USDT`). For futures USDT perpetuals, use base symbols (`BTC`, `ETH`) unless the interface or exchange data indicates otherwise.

When writing generic code, use:

```csharp
var spotSymbol = CoinWExchange.FormatSymbol("BTC", "USDT", TradingMode.Spot);
var futuresSymbol = CoinWExchange.FormatSymbol("BTC", "USDT", TradingMode.Futures);
```

Do not manually concatenate symbols in cross-exchange code. Use `SharedSymbol` with `.SharedClient`.

## Error Handling Notes

CoinW.Net returns API, network, and rate-limit errors through `.Error` on result objects. It generally does not throw for normal API failures. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

Common categories:

```text
Authentication/signature error: credentials, environment, timestamp, or permissions issue. Do not retry blindly.
Insufficient balance: permanent for current request; surface to caller.
Invalid symbol: verify CoinW symbol format and endpoint branch.
Invalid price/quantity/leverage: validate symbol rules, quantity units, and margin mode.
Rate limit/network/server issue: may be transient; retry only when Error.IsTransient is true.
Unknown order: may be expected for already-filled, canceled, or missing order ids.
```

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs
  Client setup, public ticker lookup, authenticated balances, limit spot order, order status, cancellation.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs
  Futures ticker, margin mode, market order, position lookup, close position.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Public spot ticker stream, spot kline stream, authenticated spot user streams, futures stream, teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis REST and socket patterns.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  WebCallResult pattern, transient retry, CoinW error examples, symbol formatting.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package CoinW.Net`.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

- Does the code compile with the current package?
- Does it include `using CoinW.Net;` when using credentials or environments?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does it use the correct API branch: `SpotApi` or `FuturesApi`?
- Does spot code use symbols like `BTC_USDT`?
- Does futures code use symbols like `BTC` or check `GetSymbolsAsync()`?
- Does it avoid raw CoinW URLs and manual signing?
- Does it avoid non-existent `CoinWClient`, `UsdFuturesApi`, `CoinFuturesApi`, and `GeneralApi` members?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICoinWRestClient.cs
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICoinWSocketClient.cs
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/ICoinWRestClientSpotApi*.cs
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/SpotApi/ICoinWSocketClientSpotApi*.cs
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/FuturesApi/ICoinWRestClientFuturesApi*.cs
CoinW.Net/Interfaces/Clients/FuturesApi/ICoinWSocketClientFuturesApi*.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
var client = new CoinWClient();
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new CoinWRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC_USDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
var tickers = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickersAsync();
var ticker = tickers.Data.Single(x => x.Symbol == "BTC_USDT");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
client.UsdFuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...);
```

Use:

```csharp
client.FuturesApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(...);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC");
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.FuturesApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("BTC");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```
